stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Few records survive describing the life of Italian violinist Maddalena Lombardini Sirmen (1745–1818).
  • She was trained at the Ospedale dei Mendicanti in Venice and gained regional recognition as a performer and composer.
  • She published six string quartets and several violin concertos during her career.
  • Her reputation has grown substantially in recent decades thanks to scholarly recordings and editions of her compositions by modern musicologists.
  • In 2018, the ensemble Allegra released an acclaimed recording of her quartets that reached new audiences worldwide.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the effect modern scholarship had on building Lombardini Sirmen’s reputation. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Various musicologists have studied Lombardini Sirmen, the violinist and composer trained at the Ospedale dei Mendicanti who published six string quartets.
Lombardini Sirmen’s string quartets are featured in many recordings, including the 2018 album by the ensemble Allegra.
Though she gained only regional recognition during her lifetime, Lombardini Sirmen’s reputation has grown substantially thanks to recent scholarly recordings and editions of her work.
Thanks to recent scholarship, Lombardini Sirmen was regionally recognized as a successful performer and composer trained in Venice.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the effect modern scholarship had on building Lombardini Sirmen’s reputation, noting that scholarly recordings and editions produced after her lifetime have grown her reputation substantially.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence mentions that musicologists have studied Lombardini Sirmen but doesn’t emphasize the effect this scholarship had on building her reputation. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence indicates that her quartets appear in many recordings but doesn’t emphasize the effect of these recordings on her reputation. Choice D is incorrect. While it appears to emphasize the effect of scholarship, the sentence misrepresents information from the notes. According to the notes, scholarly attention has grown her reputation substantially in recent decades, not made her regionally recognized during her lifetime.
